update:
Documentation has been added to explain the states that a cluster nodes goes
through in its lifecycle. Understanding these states helps you manage costs
and resources, minimize disruptions, and troubleshoot errors. For more
information, see  Node lifecycle .

Preview : You can use Gemini in the Google Cloud console as
an AI-powered interface to evaluate hardware options, estimate deployment costs,
and view recommended configurations for your clusters. Interacting with
Gemini helps you reach an optimal configuration for your cluster
before you create or modify it. For more information, see
 Design and optimize your cluster with Gemini .

Generally available : You can temporarily mount Cloud Storage buckets on
cluster nodes for the duration of a Slurm job. This action helps you read and
write data directly to Cloud Storage without permanently adding storage
resources to your cluster. For more information, see
 Mount Cloud Storage buckets for specific jobs .

Generally available : Cluster Director supports the following regions: 
 
 APAC : 
 
 Tokyo, Japan ( asia-northeast1 ) 
 Delhi, India ( asia-south2 ) 
 
 North America : 
 
 Los Angeles, USA ( us-west2 ) 
 Salt Lake City, USA ( us-west3 ) 
 
 
 For more information, see
 Cluster Director locations .

Preview : Before you create Spot VMs in a cluster partition, you can
view the real-time availability, estimated uptime, historical preemption rate,
and pricing for a specific machine type and location. This information helps you
maximize the chances of successfully creating Spot VMs and choose
the configuration that best fits your workload needs and budget. For more
information, see
 Use Spot .

Documentation has been added to explain the lifecycle for the supported OS image
families in Cluster Director. For more information, see
 Image family lifecycle .

Documentation has been added to list the cluster properties that restart or
recreate nodes when you modify a cluster. This information helps you minimize
disruptions to your workload. For more information, see
 Modify a cluster .

Generally available : Every two minutes, Slurm runs a background health check
on the GPUs attached to your compute nodes to detect errors. If the script finds
an error, then it logs the error to the compute node's system logs. This check
helps you monitor your GPUs without draining your nodes. For more information,
see
 Key Slurm capabilities .

Documentation has been added to list the available OS image families in
Cluster Director, their end of support (EOS) dates, and their patch
release notes. For more information, see
 Image family release notes .

Cluster operations fail if you use a gcloud CLI version earlier than
550.0.0. To resolve this issue, update the gcloud CLI to version
550.0.0 or later. For more information, see
 Known issues .

Cluster Director  is generally available
( GA ).
